title: The Delivery

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm pregnant. Great.
jokes.setup: Pam casually mentions being pregnant as an afterthought to business call
jokes.punchline: The flat 'Great.' delivery treating pregnancy like a minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character-appropriate understatement of major life event

jokes.text: My cousin came down with a case of that nasty new goat fungus. Oh, it's just horrific. The doctor says he's never seen it beard so quickly.
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to use personal story for sales sympathy
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up 'goat fungus' that affects beards
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd fake disease with specific medical terminology - pure Dwight

jokes.text: I need a baby. I'll never outsell jim and pam without one. Also, I've been noticing a gaping hole in my life.
jokes.setup: Dwight's competitive nature with Jim and Pam
jokes.punchline: Reducing having a baby to a sales advantage, then tacking on emotional need
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - competitive reasoning first, emotions second

jokes.text: Sometimes I wake up cradling a gourd.
jokes.setup: Dwight describing his emotional emptiness
jokes.punchline: The specific, bizarre image of unconsciously cuddling produce
jokes.explanation: Instant classic - perfect economy, absurd specificity, deeply weird visual

jokes.text: Kevin, you're such a gourmand.
jokes.setup: Kevin's eating habits being discussed
jokes.punchline: Using sophisticated culinary term for Kevin's simple gluttony
jokes.explanation: Mild irony but not particularly sharp or memorable

jokes.text: Last night I had edward's cornflake chicken.
jokes.setup: Kevin cooking through Twilight cookbook
jokes.punchline: Treating vampire character's food preferences as real recipes
jokes.explanation: Funny concept but execution is straightforward

jokes.text: Not all meals, just second breakfast, lunch, second lunch, and first dinner.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining his meal schedule with pregnant Pam
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous number and naming of meals
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with hobbit-like meal naming, perfect for Kevin

jokes.text: If it's crying all night, one of us is gonna have to take care of it. And I do not plan on helping unless it's a boy.
jokes.setup: Jim discussing baby care responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Casually announcing sexist parenting plan
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that subverts Jim's usual character - moderately effective

jokes.text: 'contraptions'! She's 'contrapting.'
jokes.setup: Pam having contractions
jokes.punchline: Michael's malapropism turning medical term into nonsense
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael wordplay but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Actually, I put diesel in this time... Try to save some money.
jokes.setup: Michael offering to drive them to hospital
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning he put wrong fuel in car
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael incompetence delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Well, the baby was conceived here, so it might as well live here a little bit too.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his baby-proofing of the office
jokes.punchline: Bizarre logic about where baby should live
jokes.explanation: Michael's weird logic but not his sharpest moment

jokes.text: Burning man, port-a-potty.
jokes.setup: Michael saying baby was conceived in office
jokes.punchline: Jim revealing the gross actual conception location
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and maximum gross-out factor with just three words

jokes.text: Ooh! Yuck! T.M.I. How was it? I don't want to know. Tell me later.
jokes.setup: Jim's TMI revelation about conception
jokes.punchline: Michael's contradictory reactions in rapid succession
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Michael's inability to process information

jokes.text: Otherwise, the baby's gonna become a teenager in there, and you'll be up all night from the rock music.
jokes.setup: Warning Pam not to wait too long
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of baby aging in womb and playing music
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist imagery typical of Michael's thought process

jokes.text: Shut up, nick. God! What a weird thing to say, weird I.T. Nerd. Don't get revenge on me, nerd.
jokes.setup: Michael being corrected or challenged
jokes.punchline: Unprovoked attack on random IT guy with immediate paranoia
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael meanness followed by immediate fear of consequences

jokes.text: Bear my child. Excuse me? I want to have a child for business reasons, and I want you to be the mother. If you agree, say nothing. If you disagree, say anything.
jokes.setup: Dwight's need for a baby established earlier
jokes.punchline: Completely transactional proposal with backwards consent logic
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted Dwight moment - bizarre proposal with his signature logical structure

jokes.text: Very well. Let's meet at 4:00 p.M. At our old meeting spot and bang it out.
jokes.setup: Angela's silence interpreted as consent
jokes.punchline: Dwight's business-like scheduling of sex with crude terminology
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up maintaining Dwight's matter-of-fact approach to intimacy

jokes.text: if your baby's born tomorrow, He's gonna have the same birthday as buttmud brooks, My old roommate.
jokes.setup: Discussion about timing of baby's birth
jokes.punchline: Kevin's concern about sharing birthday with absurdly named person
jokes.explanation: Buttmud Brooks is an instant classic absurd name, perfect Kevin energy

jokes.text: I'm just the left tackle who happened to get her pregnant.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining his role in birth decisions
jokes.punchline: Reducing his parental role to a football position
jokes.explanation: Good sports metaphor that captures Jim's supportive but secondary role

jokes.text: My brother was born, and my parents felt he better exemplified the walter jr. Name, so they gave it to him. I was given 'andrew,' which they got out of a baby-name book.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his name change as a child
jokes.punchline: Parents deciding sibling was better fit for his original name
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly sad backstory that explains so much about Andy's character

jokes.text: When I was a kid, my mom received compliments left and right from my teachers on how... I was always able to distract others in class.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming to be master of distraction
jokes.punchline: Reframing being disruptive student as positive skill
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael self-delusion about negative traits

jokes.text: [imitating flatulence]
jokes.setup: Michael trying to distract with trivia question
jokes.punchline: Resorting to childish sound effects
jokes.explanation: Lazy toilet humor, though it fits Michael's immaturity

jokes.text: Oh, my god, pam, you are a woman warrior.
jokes.setup: Pam having contractions during meeting
jokes.punchline: Kelly's over-the-top dramatic praise
jokes.explanation: Very Kelly response but not particularly funny

jokes.text: I can put on lipstick the way molly ringwald does in the breakfast club.
jokes.setup: Michael asking for special skills to distract Pam
jokes.punchline: Phyllis offering very specific, useless pop culture reference skill
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific skill that's perfectly Phyllis

jokes.text: Nope. No, I don't think anybody wants to see that.
jokes.setup: Phyllis offering her lipstick skill
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate shutdown
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction but effective timing

jokes.text: You're clapping. I need complete silence. Totally threw me off, so I'm gonna have to start over.
jokes.setup: Creed about to perform evolution of dance
jokes.punchline: Blaming audience for his own inability to perform
jokes.explanation: Classic Creed - making excuses for not delivering on promises

jokes.text: Child will be breast-fed by the mother for exactly six months, then weaned onto a nutrient-rich winter-vegetable mash provided by the father, dwight schrute, hereafter referred to as morpheus.
jokes.setup: Dwight presenting parenting contract
jokes.punchline: Overly specific feeding plan with Matrix reference name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - combining agricultural knowledge with pop culture reference

jokes.text: Think of pam's nipples as toby's grundle.
jokes.setup: Michael reading labor induction tips, saying not to stimulate nipples
jokes.punchline: Comparing Pam's anatomy to Toby's least appealing body part
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriateness combining his hatred of Toby with TMI

jokes.text: I have a shirt like that in my car.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to cut holes in Pam's shirt
jokes.punchline: Creed casually admitting to having prepared clothing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disturbing Creed moment - implying terrible things with few words

jokes.text: Stick spicy food up her butt.
jokes.setup: Michael asking for opposite of eating spicy food
jokes.punchline: Kevin's literal but inappropriate interpretation
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that fits Kevin's crude logic but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I'm not crazy, she's crazy. 'five to seven minutes.' 'six minutes.' different, but not really. 'five to seven minutes.'
jokes.setup: Jim getting stressed about Pam's timing
jokes.punchline: His increasingly frantic analysis of minute differences
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jim's rare loss of composure

jokes.text: It's as simple as keeping the womb extremely warm for two days after sex and then extremely cold for five months.
jokes.setup: Angela saying he can't control baby's gender
jokes.punchline: Dwight's confident pseudo-scientific method
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight fake expertise with ridiculous specificity

jokes.text: I called the doctor, like, a week ago. I couldn't wait.
jokes.setup: Jim asking if Pam said 'she' about the baby
jokes.punchline: Pam sheepishly admitting she found out the gender
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment but not particularly comedic

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. My water broke.
jokes.setup: Jim asking if she changed clothes
jokes.punchline: Pam's casual mention of major childbirth milestone
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing on major plot development

jokes.text: Too bad you didn't have sex, like, 7,5 hours later. But you had to have the afternoon delight.
jokes.setup: Pam almost making it to midnight for insurance
jokes.punchline: Michael commenting inappropriately on their sex timing
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate overshare moment

jokes.text: We have ratatouille from ratatouille and tandoori chicken from born into brothels.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining ultra-feast Hollywood theme
jokes.punchline: Pairing wholesome Pixar movie with disturbing documentary
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in Kevin's innocent movie-themed menu planning

jokes.text: I can't do it. I don't think I can do it.
jokes.setup: Everyone pressuring Pam to go to hospital
jokes.punchline: Pam's emotional breakdown about her fears
jokes.explanation: More dramatic than comedic - necessary character moment

jokes.text: Should I bring a dictionary to the hospital? The hospital will provide dictionaries. Bring a thesaurus.
jokes.setup: Michael panicking about what to bring
jokes.punchline: Dwight's equally absurd but more specific suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael/Dwight dynamic with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: I didn't know we had a tape measure. We don't.
jokes.setup: Dwight offering to check how dilated Pam is
jokes.punchline: Dwight thinking he needs a tape measure for internal exam
jokes.explanation: Good misunderstanding of medical terminology

jokes.text: I put an ad in the paper starting an escort service. Got a lot of responses... Mostly creeps. Made a few friends.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining he loves escorting people
jokes.punchline: His innocent misunderstanding of 'escort service' with dark implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight moment - innocent intentions with disturbing implications

jokes.text: Then why didn't you ask me to do it yesterday? I kept I.M.Ing you how bored I was.
jokes.setup: Michael saying they need the iPod 'yesterday'
jokes.punchline: Dwight's literal interpretation and complaint about being ignored
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight literal-mindedness with touch of pathos

jokes.text: I just did.
jokes.setup: Guard saying he can't park in restricted area
jokes.punchline: Michael's defiant response after already parking
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Michael entitlement moment

jokes.text: she's ten inches dilated now... Sorry, meters... centimeters.
jokes.setup: Jim giving medical update to camera
jokes.punchline: His confusion about measurement units for dilation
jokes.explanation: Good nervous dad moment showing Jim's medical ignorance

jokes.text: Nope, not yet, not yet, not yet. Gotta go wash my eyes. That kid's gonna have a lot of hair.
jokes.setup: Michael witnessing the birth
jokes.punchline: His traumatic reaction and inappropriate observation
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate reaction to intimate moment

jokes.text: She was supposed to come out yesterday. I decided to give baby halpert a newspaper from the day she was born. This frame set me back 55 bones.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his gift plan
jokes.punchline: His frustration that baby didn't cooperate with his schedule
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael self-centeredness about others' major life events

jokes.text: I have an ice cream cake in the car.
jokes.setup: Phyllis saying they can't wait around
jokes.punchline: Revealing her practical concern about melting cake
jokes.explanation: Good Phyllis moment - practical concern disguised as impatience

jokes.text: [imitating james cagney] yeah... That's right. It's a baby, see...
jokes.setup: Baby being born, emotional moment
jokes.punchline: Michael doing inappropriate gangster impression
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate timing for impressions

jokes.text: I'm sure there's still 12 of each.
jokes.setup: Pam wanting to count baby's fingers and toes again
jokes.punchline: Jim's mathematical error about normal digit count
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from tired new dad making basic mistake

jokes.text: I am a diapering master. I've done little else in the past two months. There is nothing I cannot diaper. Go ahead. Try to think of something. I dare you.
jokes.setup: Dwight offering to change baby's diaper
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top confidence and challenge to others
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight overconfidence with bizarre specific skill

jokes.text: That's so weird. She was saying it just before you got here.
jokes.setup: Baby making sounds when Michael arrives
jokes.punchline: Jim claiming baby was saying Michael's name
jokes.explanation: Sweet Jim prank on emotional Michael

jokes.text: What I did find... Was mold and lots of it. So I did what anyone would do. I got a good night's sleep and made plans to eradicate it.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his search for Pam's iPod
jokes.punchline: Finding mold and treating it like military operation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight overreaction to household issue

jokes.text: The odds of them getting together were 'insurmountainable.' I made a family. I got these two together, and I made a family.
jokes.setup: Michael taking credit for Jim and Pam's relationship
jokes.punchline: Malapropism creating nonsense word while claiming credit
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael word confusion while taking undeserved credit

jokes.text: who wants to live in a world where stanley has two lovers and you don't have any?
jokes.setup: Michael offering matchmaking services
jokes.punchline: Using Stanley's success as motivation for others
jokes.explanation: Good motivational logic using Stanley as unlikely romantic success

jokes.text: Torn scrotum... still on the mend, so... Not good timing.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Andrew about dating
jokes.punchline: Andy's TMI medical excuse
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy oversharing with uncomfortable detail

jokes.text: I have a small skylight in my bedroom, and I'd like for the moon to be visible.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining when he'll ask out Erin
jokes.punchline: His romantic but overly specific astronomical requirement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - romantic gesture made unnecessarily complicated

jokes.text: I am going to fill the empty voids in your life with love. I am going to fill that empty hole in your body with another person.
jokes.setup: Michael's motivational matchmaking speech
jokes.punchline: Increasingly sexual metaphors he doesn't realize he's making
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael accidental innuendo escalation

jokes.text: Kevin has an elephant heart. Literally, he has an elephant heart. He had a transplant, when he was 17... No. Kevin doesn't have an elephant heart.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining Kevin's sensitivity
jokes.punchline: Elaborate lie followed by immediate confession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael manipulation technique - elaborate lie with instant reversal

jokes.text: Why was I hiding behind the plant? well, in college, I took a botany class, and there was lots of drama in that class... And then I would jump out at them and confront them and be like, 'oh, you think all I do is hide behind plants and spy on people?' busted.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why he was behind plant
jokes.punchline: Completely irrelevant college story that proves their point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy rambling that completely undermines his own defense

jokes.text: 'got milk?' [chuckles]
jokes.setup: Male lactation consultant introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Using outdated ad slogan as professional joke
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that's more awkward than funny

jokes.text: Why don't you meet me here at exactly mid-late afternoon?
jokes.setup: Dwight scheduling meeting with Angela
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical time specification
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight precision applied to imprecise concept

jokes.text: Do you really think that you could have dated erin? You said she liked me. Okay, even if someone told you that, You should know that that could never be possible, kevin.
jokes.setup: Michael breaking news to Kevin about Erin
jokes.punchline: His brutally honest assessment of Kevin's dating prospects
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael cruelty disguised as honesty

jokes.text: Wrong baby. Wrong baby! This is not our baby!
jokes.setup: Pam holding what she thinks is her baby
jokes.punchline: Discovering they have the wrong infant
jokes.explanation: Great escalating panic over major parental nightmare

jokes.text: How some people spend their money. Right?
jokes.setup: Isabel mentioning adults should go to dentist
jokes.punchline: Dwight's comment while having renovated someone else's kitchen
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony with Dwight criticizing spending while having just renovated Jim's house

jokes.text: The fax says... 'erin, will you have dinner with me? From andy.' And the number is our office fax number.
jokes.setup: Andy asking Erin to send urgent fax
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's just his awkward way of asking her out
jokes.explanation: Sweet Andy gesture that's unnecessarily complicated in typical fashion

jokes.text: Just give me a couple days... Be out of your hair.
jokes.setup: Dwight's extreme kitchen renovation
jokes.punchline: Casual mention he's now living in their house
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Dwight has moved in while 'fixing' their mold problem

tmdb_overview: Sabre's CEO wants the Scranton branch to have one manager, and Michael voluntarily steps down and lets Jim become the boss when he finds out that salespeople make more money. However, he soon comes to regret the decision.
